name: Es Cap de Barbaria and Torre des Garroveret - circular gravel in the Ses Salines Natural Park
name_original: Es Cap de Barbaria y Torre des Garroveret — circular gravel por el Parque Natural de Ses Salines
description: This difficult 22.3-mile touring cycling route explores Es Cap de Barbaria and Torre des Garroveret in Ses Salines Natural Park.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Formentera is characterized by its largely flat topography, making it accessible for various skill levels. The island features an extensive network of "green routes" that utilize old rural roads, providing an eco-friendly way to explore its diverse landscapes. These routes often traverse coastal areas, salt flats, and rural interiors with traditional cultivations. The terrain includes minimal elevation changes, with some routes offering views from low hills. Best touring cycling routes around Formentera The most popular touring cycling route is El Pilar de la Mola Loop, a 21.3 miles (34.2 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 37 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant elevation gain, leading to panoramic views from the La Mola plateau. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Es Cap de Barbaria and Torre des Garroveret - circular gravel in the Ses Salines Natural Park, a difficult 22.3 miles (35.8 km) path. This route explores the protected Ses Salines Natural Park, featuring gravel sections and coastal scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the View of Formentera – La Mola Lighthouse loop from Platja de Migjorn, a 18.3 miles (29.5 km) trail leading through coastal areas and towards the iconic Faro de la Mola, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. Touring cycling around Formentera is defined by its green routes, salt lagoons, and lighthouses. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal paths to more challenging loops with moderate climbs. The routes in Formentera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Formentera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Formentera?
A: Formentera offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 110 options available. These routes cater to various preferences, ranging from easy coastal paths to more challenging loops with moderate climbs.

Q: What makes Formentera ideal for touring cycling?
A: Formentera is exceptional for touring cycling due to its largely flat topography, making it accessible for all skill levels. The island boasts an extensive network of 32 'green routes,' with 29 suitable for cycling, totaling over 100 kilometers. These routes often utilize old, safe rural roads, allowing for an eco-friendly exploration of diverse landscapes, from coastal areas and salt flats to rural interiors.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Formentera is very family-friendly and offers many easy routes. The island's mostly flat terrain is ideal for beginners and families with children. Over 75 of the available touring cycling routes are classified as easy, ensuring a comfortable and enjoyable experience for everyone.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Formentera?
A: Absolutely. Many of Formentera's touring cycling routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38178532" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ses Illetes Beach – La Savina Port loop from la Savina&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate ride exploring the northern part of the island.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I see on the touring cycling routes?
A: Cycling in Formentera offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll traverse rural interiors with traditional almond, fig, and grape cultivations, juniper forests, and arid areas. Routes also lead to stunning white-sand beaches like Platja de Illetes and Cala Saona, salt lagoons, and dramatic coastal views from high cliffs, especially around the island's lighthouses.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the Ses Salines Natural Park?
A: Yes, you can explore the protected Ses Salines Natural Park by bike. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008531045/es-cap-de-barbaria-and-torre-des-garroveret-circular-gravel-in-the-ses-salines-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Es Cap de Barbaria and Torre des Garroveret - circular gravel in the Ses Salines Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; route is a great option, featuring gravel sections and coastal scenery within this biodiverse area.

Q: Which routes offer views of the iconic lighthouses?
A: Formentera's lighthouses are popular cycling destinations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40316330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Formentera – La Mola Lighthouse loop from Platja de Migjorn&lt;/a&gt; leads towards the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/601057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, offering spectacular panoramic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008543414/el-pilar-de-la-mola-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pilar de la Mola Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which also provides rewarding rides with dramatic coastal scenery.

Q: What are some interesting places to explore or landmarks to see along the routes?
A: Along Formentera's routes, you can discover several interesting landmarks and natural features. Don't miss the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/601057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2995920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbària Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; for stunning coastal views. You can also visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/451817" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ses Portes Tower&lt;/a&gt;, the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/448087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ses Salines Beach&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/730372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foradada Cave&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5389257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt Flats of Ibiza&lt;/a&gt; are also a notable natural monument to observe.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Formentera?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Formentera is typically during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, the weather is pleasantly mild, and the island is less crowded than in peak summer, offering ideal conditions for exploring the green routes and enjoying the natural beauty.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that feature salt lagoons?
A: Yes, you can cycle alongside beautiful salt lagoons in Formentera. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5300588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Formentera – Salt Lagoon loop from Molí d’en Botigues&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that borders the picturesque Estany Pudent within the Ses Salines Natural Park, offering opportunities for birdwatching and views of the pink salt flats.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Formentera?
A: The komoot community highly rates touring cycling in Formentera, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 300 reviews. Cyclists often praise the island's extensive network of 'green routes,' the mostly flat and accessible terrain, and the opportunity to discover pristine landscapes and charming villages away from motorized traffic. The sustainable and authentic experience of connecting with the island's natural environment is also a frequently highlighted aspect.
tips: Quiet and scenic gravel path along the bay, perfect for picnic too!
Great alternative to mainroad for a quieter ride toward the southern lighthouse of Formentera.
One of the most iconic places in Formentera with the image of an almost desert landscape, a narrow road, the distant lighthouse in the center and the blue sea in the background.

Old defense tower built in the 18th century to protect the island and which served as a means of sighting enemy ships. Following the path you can also reach the Cap de Barbaria lighthouse located further south.
At the westernmost side there's a dead end sign, but this doesn't count for bikes. Passage is totally fine and on a nice gravel road, too.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this circular gravel route?
faqs.answer: This route is approximately 35.8 kilometers long and is estimated to take around 2 hours and 39 minutes to complete on a touring bicycle. Keep in mind that this is an estimate, and your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is a diverse mix, featuring both asphalt and gravel tracks. You should be prepared for rougher sections, sand, and cliffside paths. Some parts might even require you to push your bike due to soft or challenging surfaces. For this reason, a mountain bike or hybrid bicycle is more suitable than a standard road bike.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as 'difficult' and requires good fitness. It's not recommended for beginners or families with young children due to its challenging terrain, including rough sections and potential soft sand, and its overall length and elevation gain. Formentera offers many easier 'Green Routes' for less experienced cyclists.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter breathtaking scenery and notable landmarks. Highlights include cycling through Gravel around the bay and Gravel backroads to lighthouse. You'll pass the iconic Cap de Barbaria Lighthouse and the historic Old defense tower Torre des Garroveret. The route also features Gravel roads for bicycle passage, dramatic sea cliffs, pine woods, salt flats, and panoramic views of La Savina port and Estany des Peix.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: To enjoy the most pleasant weather and avoid the peak summer heat, the best time to cycle this route is during the spring (April-May) or autumn (September-October). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural landscape is often at its most vibrant.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Ses Salines Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on designated routes within Ses Salines Natural Park in Formentera. However, as it is a protected natural area, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and avoid disturbing wildlife or vegetation.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While Formentera's natural areas are generally welcoming, specific regulations for dogs on cycling trails within Ses Salines Natural Park can vary. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. Be prepared to carry water for your dog, as sources may be limited.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for this exact starting point isn't detailed, but as a circular route, you can typically begin and end at various points. For Cap de Barbaria, there is often parking available near the lighthouse, though it can get busy. For other sections within Ses Salines Natural Park, look for designated parking areas near the 'Green Routes' access points.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see in Ses Salines Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Ses Salines Natural Park is an important ecological site, especially for birdwatching. You might spot various species such as flamingos, ducks, and seagulls, particularly around the Estany Pudent saltwater lagoon. The pine woods and coastal areas also host diverse flora and fauna.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several official trails. You will find yourself on parts of El Camí IT4TR6 El Camí Illenc: de l'Alguer a Formentera, as well as segments of the Camí des Pujols, Camí de Can Mayans d'es Pujols, and Camí de les Cordes. It also briefly touches Carrer de L'Arxiduc Lluís Salvador, Passatge del Lalaut Verd, and Camí de Riera.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this challenging touring bicycle ride?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, it's essential to pack appropriately. Bring plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), a basic bike repair kit (spare tube, pump), and a first-aid kit. Wearing appropriate cycling attire and sturdy shoes is also highly recommended.

waypoints.name: Old defense tower Torre des Garroveret
